summary.intent: Address Codex code review findings on PR #669 (ars-ui combobox) by fixing three P1 bugs related to controlled state synchronization, then push the fixes and trigger a new review cycle.
summary.outcome: All three P1 findings were fixed with TDD (failing tests first, then implementation), CI passed, and the changes were committed as a new fix commit after resolving the prior review threads and triggering a fresh Codex review pass.

repo: The combobox component lives in crates/ars-components/src/selection/combobox/mod.rs and is the primary focus of review activity on PR #669.
Codex review state is tracked via GitHub PR reactions (👀 = in progress) and unresolved review threads filtered by author login matching 'codex'.
The review-loop skill handles the full GitHub handoff: reply to threads with fixing commit SHA, resolve threads via GraphQL mutation, then post '@codex review' to trigger the next pass.
Controlled bindable state (Bindable type in ars-core) requires explicit sync alongside optimistic selection state — the shared selection write path was not propagating values to controlled bindables.
Async item pruning (when a refreshed collection removes the selected item) must also clear the single-select input field, not just the selection state.

code.finding: Controlled clear sync path — clearing selection must write to controlled bindable, not just internal state
SyncProps / apply_selection_plan area — controlled custom commit must preserve the committed custom key when syncing
Async pruning path — when selected item is removed from collection, single-select input must be cleared
Controlled selection write path shared across clear, prune, and custom commit — all three bugs stemmed from this shared path not updating controlled bindables

workflow: TDD checkpoint is enforced: new regression tests must be written and confirmed failing before the implementation fix is applied.
Gate before each commit: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -p ars-components --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings, cargo llvm-cov test, git diff --check.
Cargo test does not support multiple --exact test name filters in a single invocation; use the module path filter instead.
GitHub review thread resolution requires two separate API calls: POST to reply with the fixing commit SHA, then GraphQL mutation to resolve the thread.
Codex review progress is polled at 120-second intervals by checking PR reactions and unresolved thread count via GraphQL.

friction: cargo test with multiple space-separated test names is invalid — the command silently failed or errored, requiring a fallback to module-level test filtering.
cargo fmt --check flagged formatting in newly added test assertions, requiring an extra fmt pass before the gate could proceed.
Codex review took multiple 120-second polling cycles (at least 5+ minutes) before producing findings, with no way to distinguish 'still reviewing' from 'stuck'.
Coverage compilation added significant wall-clock time to the gate, blocking the commit while clippy had already passed.
open_items: Codex may produce additional review findings in the next pass following commit 1043d72a — the loop exits only when Codex posts no new unresolved threads.
CI checks were still running at session end; final green status on the new push was not confirmed.
The controlled bindable sync pattern is a recurring source of bugs (this is at least the second review pass fixing related issues) — a more systematic audit of all write paths may be warranted.
Co-authorship attribution to OpenAI Codex is included in commits; the team should verify this is the intended convention for AI-assisted fixes.
